title: Wave-2 🔴: checkout + pricing — PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW outcomes, tier gating, payments-disabled + axe
acceptance_criteria: Pages: pricing, checkout/success. Pricing-memo @critical cases: PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW upgrade outcomes — one-shot → tier one-shot + activeTestCredits +1; pro → tier pro + credits +5 and NOT a subscription; studio → tier studio (subscription) + credits NOT incremented (stays 0); unauthenticated checkout POST → 401; payments-disabled path (NO PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW, Stripe disabled) → 404 surfaced gracefully in the UI (not a crash).

PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW is injected ONLY for the spec(s) that need it (test.use / per-spec env or request context) — NEVER globally; never hits real Stripe; NEVER asserts dollar amounts (May-2026 pivot — assert tier names/feature availability/CTA behaviour only).
Each tier-mutating case seeds its OWN dedicated unique-email user (the bypass mutates User.tier server-side and the nextauth session callback re-reads tier per request) with afterAll cleanup.
Post-upgrade gate: after free→one-shot via test flow, the user can reach the active-test surface without the upgrade prompt.
checkout.a11y.spec.ts (R3): axe on pricing + checkout/success, post-stable-state, scoped, WCAG A+AA, @functional, logged allow-list.
Single-tagged per qa-checklist.md; npm run typecheck passes; lists under playwright. Depends on the Wave-0 prod-guard fix being in place (T-01).
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of how PAYMENT_TEST_FLOW is scoped per-spec and never global
